epic.title: Payments (in-person POS + autopay), memberships & non-S4 rewards
epic.summary: Commerce for the clinic: in-person POS (Square card-present + recorded cash + gift cards), automatic recurring membership autopay via stored card-on-file, packages/series, and a rewards engine that only ever applies to non-S4 items — driving repeat visits without eroding margin or breaching advertising law. Financials stay owner-only; the ledger defers to Xero.

stories.title: Payment provider abstraction + Square adapter
stories.context: An IPaymentProvider port (authorize/capture, refund, void, tokenize, recurring, gift-card) with a Square adapter first and cash as an internal tender; no PAN stored, only tokens (ADR-0007).
stories.i_want: a payment-provider abstraction with a Square adapter and cash tender
acceptance: IPaymentProvider exposes authorize/capture, refund, void, tokenize, recurring and gift-card.
Square adapter implemented; cash is an internal tender.
No PAN is ever stored — only provider tokens.
Built on SPIKE-SQUARE findings.
detail: A payment-provider abstraction (IPaymentProvider) exposes authorize/capture, refund, void, tokenize, recurring and gift-card, with a Square adapter first and cash as an internal tender. No PAN is ever stored — only provider tokens (ADR-0007, PCI-safe).
Built on the SPIKE-SQUARE findings; keeps the platform provider-swappable.
ui_spec: No dedicated screen — surfaces through Checkout (checkout.png) and membership card-on-file capture; this story is the backend port + Square adapter.

data_model.fields: id, tenant_id, client_id, provider(square), token_ref, brand, last4, exp
id, payment_id, provider_ref, type(auth|capture|refund|void), amount, status

stories.title: In-person POS checkout (card + cash)
stories.context: Front desk takes payment in person via Square card-present or recorded cash, with receipts, partial/split, tips and surcharge config (REQ-PAY-2). Financial figures are owner-gated.
stories.i_want: to take payment in person by Square card or recorded cash with receipts and split/partial support
stories.so_that: I can check clients out at the desk
acceptance: A sale completes by Square card or recorded cash; both appear in the daily closeout.
Receipts, partial/split payments, tips and surcharge config supported.
Money figures respect the owner-only financial capability (reception sees no money totals beyond the sale).
Online one-off checkout is not exposed (deferred).
detail: Front desk takes payment in person via Square card-present or recorded cash, with receipts, partial/split payments, tips and surcharge config. Both card and cash appear in the daily closeout. Online one-off checkout is deferred; financial figures respect the owner-only capability.
The everyday till for the clinic.
ui_spec: Prototype: Checkout (checkout.png) — line items (service/retail/package), tender selection (Square card / cash / gift card), receipt, partial/split, tips; S4 items show no reward/discount controls (C9).
Reception sees the sale but not owner-only money totals.

data_model.fields: id, tenant_id, client_id, lines[]{type, ref, qty, price, gst}, total, status
data_model.notes: Lines carry the service/product schedule flag.

data_model.fields: id, invoice_id, tender(card|cash|giftcard), amount, token_ref?, tip, surcharge, at
data_model.notes: Appears in Closeout; posts to Xero (PRD-10).

stories.title: Packages/series, gift cards & client balances
stories.context: Sell/redeem packages (visits remaining) and gift cards, track client balances/credit and AR ageing (REQ-PAY-3/5).
stories.i_want: to sell and redeem packages and gift cards and track client balances
stories.so_that: clients can pre-pay and carry credit
acceptance: Package/series sale + redemption with 'visits remaining'.
Gift cards can be sold, balance-tracked and redeemed.
Client balance/credit and AR ageing tracked.
Redemptions appear in the closeout and post to Xero (PRD-10).
detail: Sell/redeem packages/series ('visits remaining') and gift cards, and track client balances/credit + AR ageing. Redemptions appear in the closeout and post to Xero.
Lets clients pre-pay courses and carry credit.
ui_spec: Prototype: Checkout (checkout.png) for sale/redeem; Memberships -> Gift cards (memb-gifts.png) for issuing/tracking gift-card balances; package 'visits remaining' on the Client 360.
Balance/credit + AR ageing visible to owner/manager.

data_model.fields: id, tenant_id, client_id, service_id, total_visits, remaining, purchased_at
id, tenant_id, code, initial, balance, status

stories.title: Daily closeout & reconciliation
stories.i_want: a daily closeout that balances card and cash
acceptance: Closeout summarises card + cash tenders for the day.
Variances are surfaced.
Closeout figures are owner-gated.
Closeout reconciles to the Xero posting (PRD-10).
detail: An end-of-day closeout balances card + cash for the day and surfaces variances. Figures are owner-gated and reconcile to the Xero posting (PRD-10).
The daily till-reconciliation routine.
ui_spec: Prototype: Checkout -> daily closeout (checkout.png) — card vs cash totals for the day, variance highlight; owner-only figures.
Also surfaced on the back-office tablet (backroom.png).

data_model.fields: id, tenant_id, location_id, date, card_total, cash_total, variance, closed_by

stories.title: Memberships with automatic autopay & dunning
stories.context: Membership plans/tiers with automatic recurring billing from a tokenised card-on-file (added online/in-app or at desk) and failed-payment dunning (REQ-MEMB-1/2/3).
stories.i_want: to join a membership and have my card auto-charged on schedule
stories.so_that: I get member perks without manual payments
acceptance: A membership auto-charges on schedule from a stored token (card added online/in-app or in person).
A failed charge triggers dunning/recovery.
Lifecycle (join/pause/cancel/win-back) tracked → MRR/churn reporting (PRD-08).
Benefits/credits auto-apply at checkout.
detail: Membership plans/tiers with automatic recurring billing from a tokenised card-on-file (added online/in-app or at the desk) and failed-payment dunning. Lifecycle (join/pause/cancel/win-back) is tracked and feeds MRR/churn reporting; benefits/credits auto-apply at checkout.
The recurring-revenue engine; autopay built on the Square recurring spike.
ui_spec: Prototype: Memberships -> Members & billing (memb-members.png) — member list, plan, card-on-file status, next charge, dunning state; Plans & packages (memb-plans.png) to define tiers.

data_model.fields: id, tenant_id, name, tier, price, period, benefits[]
id, client_id, plan_id, token_ref, schedule, status(active|paused|cancelled), next_charge_at

stories.title: Rewards engine — non-S4 only
stories.context: Visit-based + membership rewards that the engine blocks from ever applying to S4 items; configuring an S4 reward is blocked (REQ-MEMB-4/5/7, C9/ADR-0014).
stories.i_want: to earn and redeem rewards on non-S4 items only
stories.so_that: I'm rewarded without breaching S4 advertising rules
acceptance: Visit-based rewards (milestones/every-Nth-visit) + membership perks on non-S4 items, add-ons or account/gift credit.
The engine refuses to earn, redeem or discount against any S4-flagged item.
Attempting to configure an S4 reward is blocked.
Catalog schedule flag (from PRD-04) drives eligibility.
detail: Visit-based rewards (milestones/every-Nth-visit) + membership perks that the engine blocks from ever applying to S4 items; configuring an S4 reward is blocked (C9/ADR-0014). Rewards apply to non-S4 items, add-ons, or account/gift credit — never the toxin itself.
Drives repeat visits without eroding margin or breaching advertising law. The catalog schedule flag (PRD-04) drives eligibility.
ui_spec: Prototype: Memberships -> Loyalty (memb-loyalty.png) — reward rules + ledger; S4 items in the catalog show disabled reward/discount controls with a tooltip (the S4 guardrail).
Earn/redeem visible on the Client 360 + client app rewards.

data_model.fields: id, client_id, earned, redeemed, balance, ref

stories.title: Margin-aware reward rules
stories.context: Owners set value caps and eligible items; reporting shows reward-cost vs retention (REQ-MEMB-6). Reward comms respect advertising rules (C9/C23).
stories.i_want: to set margin-aware reward rules with caps and eligible items and see reward-cost vs retention
stories.so_that: rewards drive retention without eroding margin
acceptance: Reward rules enforce value caps and eligible-item lists.
Reward-cost vs retention surfaces in reporting (PRD-08).
Reward communications go only to consented, logged-in clients (no public S4 price promotion).
Rule config is owner-gated.
detail: Owners set margin-aware reward rules (value caps, eligible items) and see reward-cost vs retention in reporting. Reward communications go only to consented, logged-in clients (no public S4 price promotion) (C9/C23).
Keeps loyalty profitable and compliant; rule config is owner-gated.
ui_spec: Prototype: Memberships -> Pricing & what-if (memb-pricing.png, owner-only .fin) — caps + eligible items; reward-cost vs retention surfaces in Reports (PRD-08).

stories.title: Checkout assist & post-visit rebooking
stories.context: Subtle membership/restock upsell + client rapport panel + post-checkout rebooking on the treatment interval (REQ-PAY-6, ADR-0022).
stories.i_want: checkout to suggest relevant non-S4 upsells and prompt rebooking at the right interval
stories.so_that: I help clients and keep them on cadence
acceptance: Checkout shows a subtle membership/restock upsell and a client rapport panel.
Post-checkout rebooking is offered on the treatment interval.
Upsell suggestions never include S4 discounting.
Rebooking integrates with the calendar (PRD-02) and recall (PRD-07).
detail: At checkout, a subtle membership/restock upsell + a client-rapport panel, and a post-checkout rebooking prompt on the treatment interval. Upsell suggestions never include S4 discounting.
Helps the desk add value and keep clients on cadence; integrates with the calendar (PRD-02) and recall (PRD-07).
ui_spec: Prototype: Checkout (checkout.png) — a subtle upsell card (e.g. 'Slow-moving retail: bundle SPF with facials'), a client-rapport panel, and a rebook prompt at the interval after payment.

data_model.fields: from RewardRule + stock aging + treatment interval

stories.title: Pricing & what-if planning (owner)
stories.context: An owner pricing + what-if planner over the reporting read-models (REQ-MEMB-9, ADR-0022). The Finance screen is a light pricing + reporting hub; the ledger defers to Xero.
stories.i_want: to model pricing and run what-if scenarios
stories.so_that: I can set prices and plan membership economics
acceptance: Pricing/what-if planner uses the same read-models as reporting (PRD-08).
Scenario outputs are owner-gated.
The Finance area is a pricing + reporting hub; in-app ledger/payroll/AP/BAS tooling is out of scope (Xero).
Invoices/payments still sync to Xero from checkout.
detail: An owner pricing + what-if planner over the reporting read-models — model prices and scenarios (e.g. membership economics). The Finance area is a light pricing + reporting hub; the ledger/payroll/AP/BAS tooling defers to Xero. Invoices/payments still sync from checkout.
Scenario outputs are owner-gated.
ui_spec: Prototype: Memberships -> Pricing & what-if (memb-pricing.png) and Finance (finance.png) — pricing model + scenario sliders; owner-only.
Reads the same read-models as Reports (PRD-08).

data_model.notes: Over PRD-08 read-models; owner-gated. No in-app ledger (ADR-0027).

stories.title: Referrals & affiliate credit (non-S4) (placeholder)
stories.context: The prototype's Memberships → Referrals screen shows referral/affiliate credit. Per scope, advanced loyalty/referrals are Phase 2 and referral/affiliate credit is non-S4 only (REQ-MEMB-10).
stories.i_want: to refer friends and earn non-S4 credit
acceptance: Placeholder — Phase 2; core membership/rewards mechanics ship first.
Referral/affiliate credit is non-S4 only (no S4 incentive), reusing the rewards engine guardrail (C9).
Captured so the rewards model stays referral-ready.
detail: Placeholder (Phase 2): referrals/affiliate credit, non-S4 only — reusing the rewards-engine guardrail (no S4 incentive, C9). Core membership/rewards mechanics ship first.
Captured so the rewards model stays referral-ready.
ui_spec: Prototype: Memberships -> Referrals (memb-referrals.png) — referral/affiliate credit concept.
